commit.message:
fix: array insert no longer inherits block comments from existing elements

multiline_prefix() and save_first_prefix() were copying the first
element's entire decor prefix — including block comments — onto newly
inserted values.  This caused insert(0, x) and insert(n, x) to give
the new element a stale block comment that belonged to another element.

Now both helpers extract only the structural indentation (\n + indent),
leaving block comments attached to their original elements.

commit.message:
Improve tab completions grid rendering logic (#365)

Implements four improvements to the tab completion grid rendering:

## Changes Made

- **Early "No suggestions" exit**: When there are zero filtered
suggestions, the "No suggestions" message is printed immediately at the
top of the render block and the grid-building logic is skipped entirely.

- **Middle-ellipsis truncation for wide suggestions**: If a suggestion's
text is wider than the terminal width and it falls in the first visible
column, `render()` now applies middle truncation (e.g.
`very_long_path_name` → `very_l…ame`) instead of silently discarding the
column.

- **2D selected position**: `ActiveSuggestions` now tracks
`(selected_row, selected_col)` instead of a flat index. Up/down arrows
navigate within a column; left/right arrows jump between columns. If
navigating right would land on an empty cell in a partial last column,
the selection wraps back to column 0.

- **Column scroll offset**: A `col_scroll_offset` field ensures the
selected column is always visible. `into_grid()` accepts a `col_offset`
parameter to skip leading columns, and the offset is updated after each
render and eagerly on navigation so there is no visible lag.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Tab completions grid improvements:
> if the number of active suggestions is zero, the print the No
suggestions at the start of the render block and exit that block.
> 
> If the suggestion is too wide to fit onto a single line and we are
drawing the first column, the remove the middle part of the suggestion
and replace it with the ellipses character.
> 
> Keep track of the selected position as a 2d value, not just an index.
When you move to the right, increase the column index. The in the index
check function, if there isn't a suggestion for that 2d pos, set col to
0 etc.
> 
> If the cursor goes too far to the right, we currently wont be able to
draw this column. So we should skip some columns so that the column with
the cursor is always drawn.


</details>

commit.message:
Fix KeysView.__or__/__xor__ to accept any iterable

Python's dict_keys set operations accept any iterable, not just sets.
Our __or__ and __xor__ delegated to set.__or__/set.__xor__ which
reject non-set operands like lists and tuples.

Fix by converting the other operand to a set first via iterable_to_pyset
helper before calling the set method.

commit.message:
Refactor subscript dispatch with SubscriptKey enum

Replace the mixed key/item dispatch in __getitem__, __setitem__, and
__delitem__ with a unified SubscriptKey enum that resolves proxy keys
and classifies key type (Str/Int/Slice/Other) once before borrowing
the document.

- Add SubscriptKey enum and resolve_subscript_key() in item_ops
- Add subscript_type_error() helper for wrong-key-type errors

commit.message:
Preserve array boundary spacing on insert/append/extend

When appending, extending, or inserting at the end of an array, the
old last element's suffix (space before `]`) was left in place,
producing corrupted output like `[1, 2 , 3]`. Fix by stripping the
suffix before mutation and reapplying it to the new last element.

When inserting at index 0, the old first element's prefix (space
after `[`) was lost. Fix by copying it to the new first element;
the old prefix naturally becomes inter-element spacing.

Extract value_prefix/value_suffix helpers to deduplicate the
repeated decor-reading pattern across boundary helpers and
save_removal_decor.

commit.message:
Replace shift tracking with per-element stamping in MutationTrie

Remove the shifted_from/shifts mechanism from TrieNode entirely. Array
mutations now stamp each affected index individually via stamp_child,
matching the O(n) cost toml_edit already pays to shift elements.

This eliminates two false-staleness bugs:
- stamp_shift min-compression lost precision when two shifts had
  increasing thresholds (min collapsed them)
- stamp_child did not clear shifted_from, so old shift metadata
  poisoned later shifts after an array was replaced

The trie now has just two operations: stamp and stamp_child.
Affected::Shift(usize) becomes Affected::Range { from, to }.

commit.message:
fix: add staleness checks to KeysView, ValuesView, ItemsView

Views now store a creation revision and check the mutation trie before
every access, matching the existing ItemProxy staleness model.

Previously, accessing a view whose path had been replaced (e.g.
`view = doc["foo"].keys(); doc["foo"] = "bar"; list(view)`)
raised a confusing TypeError ("string does not support keys()").
Now it raises RuntimeError("stale"), consistent with how proxies
behave.

The staleness check is unified in Document.check_fresh(), used by both
ItemProxy and views, with a single consistent error message.

Root-level views (doc.keys()) remain live for child mutations since
the root path is never invalidated — only doc.clear() makes them stale.

commit.message:
refactor: deduplicate array removal logic in list_ops

item_remove_at is now the single implementation for removing an element
from an array-like TOML item (Array or ArrayOfTables) with correct
decor handling.

- list_pop delegates to item_remove_at after resolving the index.
- item_delitem (Array/AOT branches) delegates via as_array_like_mut,
  collapsing two match arms into one.

commit.message:
Fix window close hanging on some PCs

Move disk I/O out of OnClosing so the window disappears instantly.
Window bounds are captured in-memory during OnClosing, then persisted
in ShutdownRequested via Task.Run (avoids UI-thread deadlock when
_writeLock is held by an in-flight save). Both SaveAsync and
CopilotService.DisposeAsync now have timeouts to prevent infinite hangs.

commit.message:
Add 12 platform-specific IPC examples (#64)

* Add file-watcher example with inotify-backed server and tests

Linux directory watcher using inotify that demonstrates IPC notifications.
Server watches directories and broadcasts FileChanged events to clients.
Seven integration tests cover watch/unwatch lifecycle, event notifications
for file creation/modification/deletion, and error handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add sysmon example with /proc-based CPU, memory, and load monitoring

Server reads /proc/stat, /proc/meminfo, and /proc/loadavg to implement
GetCpuUsage, GetMemoryInfo, and GetLoadAverage RPCs. A monitoring thread
checks thresholds every second and fires HighCpuAlert/LowMemoryAlert
notifications.

Client queries all three methods in a formatted table and listens for
threshold notifications. Test suite covers all RPCs, both notification
types, invalid method handling, and RunLoop dispatch mode (8 tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add disk-usage example with simulated volumes

Implement the disk-usage example under examples/macos/disk-usage/:

- disk_device.cpp: Simulated server with 3 volumes (/, /home, /tmp).
  Usage on / grows 1 GB every 3s; fires DiskSpaceLow when > 90%.
- disk_client.cpp: Interactive CLI with list, check <mount>, quit
  commands and bar-chart display. Prints notification alerts.
- disk_test.cpp: 8 Google Test cases covering GetVolumeCount,
  GetVolumeAt (valid/invalid), GetDiskUsage (known/unknown mount),
  DiskSpaceLow notification, UsedPercent consistency, RunLoop dispatch.
- CMakeLists.txt: Builds device, client, and test executables.
- README.md: IDL overview, build/run instructions, sample output.

All volumes are simulated — no platform-specific APIs — so the example
compiles and runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add audio-dsp example: simulated DSP effects processor

Implement server (dsp_device), client CLI (dsp_dashboard), and 12 Google
Test cases covering slot management, parameter updates, effect counting,
signal levels, invalid-slot errors, and ClipDetected/LevelUpdate
notifications.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add can-bus-ecu example: simulated ECU with OBD-II PIDs

Implement ecu_device (server with state machine simulation),
ecu_dashboard (interactive CLI client), and ecu_test (11 Google
Test cases covering all RPC methods, notifications, and RunLoop
dispatch). Cross-platform — compiles on Linux and Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add BLE Heart Rate Monitor peripheral example

Implement simulated BLE HRM with GATT service model:
- ble_device: server with Heart Rate (0x180D) and Battery (0x180F)
  services, simulation thread for HR/battery updates
- ble_dashboard: interactive CLI client with service discovery,
  characteristic read, and notification subscription
- ble_test: 9 Google Test cases covering service count, get/read/
  write characteristics, notification enable/disable, error cases

All code is cross-platform (no macOS-specific APIs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add motor-controller example with simulated stepper motor

Implements MotorController IPC service with trapezoidal motion profiles,
limit switch detection, stall monitoring, and homing sequence. Includes:

- motor_device: simulated stepper motor server (10ms tick loop)
- motor_dashboard: interactive CLI client with status display
- motor_test: 10 Google Test cases covering all methods and notifications

All code is cross-platform (no Windows-specific APIs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CMake wiring and build warnings for platform examples

- Consolidate platform subdirectory includes in examples/CMakeLists.txt
- Guard Linux-only examples (sysmon, file-watcher) inside linux/CMakeLists.txt
- Remove duplicate add_subdirectory entries from parallel agent commits
- Fix -Werror=unused-result warnings in filewatcher_test.cpp

* Address code review findings across platform examples

- Fix dead code and wrong formula in battery_device timeToEmpty calc
- Move notifyXxx calls outside mutex locks in motor, gpio, procmon, svcstat
- Add can-bus-ecu to windows/CMakeLists.txt (was missing)
- Replace fragile reference capture in ble_device with member m_simRunning
- Add explicit null termination after strncpy in disk_device
- Move ecu_device printf inside lock scope to avoid data race

* Fix motor simulation crossover on coarse-timer platforms

On Windows (~15ms timer resolution) and macOS, a single simulation
tick can overshoot the MoveTo target position. Add post-update
crossover detection in both the production device and test device
to snap to target when position crosses or reaches the destination.

Fixes CI failures: MoveTo_ReachesTarget, MoveWithoutHome_Allowed
